text:
Market today: The mood is tense, driven by renewed geopolitical risk. Trump's proposed Hormuz blockade and tolls are spiking oil prices, which could fuel inflation. This isn't great for your tech/chip names, as higher inflation often means higher rates, making growth stocks less attractive.

Your holdings: SOXL (-36.03%) and SPAL (-35.75%) are bleeding heavily. As leveraged ETFs, they decay and drops are amplified by margin, demanding immediate attention. PTNM (-15.25%) is nearing its stop at 9.5, and QQQI (-1.83%) is also close to its 52 stop.

Watch: Keep a close eye on oil prices and any further inflation data. Also, monitor your stops on QQQI and PTNM, they're getting tight.

Bottom line: Geopolitical risk is back, so manage your leveraged positions aggressively and respect your stops.
plays: SOXL: This leveraged ETF is down -36.03%. Respect its volatility and decay; consider trimming or exiting to protect capital.
SPAL: Another leveraged ETF, down -35.75%. Re-evaluate your stop and risk here, as margin amplifies drops.
QQQ: Scanner shows a BUY setup. Consider adding to your QQQ position at entry 710.98-710.27, with a stop at 708.18.
